name: ogentic-shield
description: Detect attorney-client privilege, HIPAA PHI, and financial MNPI in text — locally, before content reaches an AI model.
long_description: Ogentic Shield extends Microsoft Presidio with 30 domain-specific recognizers across four profiles (legal, therapy, therapy-pro, finance) plus a 4-layer detection pipeline (regex / NER / rules / localhost-Ollama LLM). All processing runs on your device. The MCP bundle exposes five tools — `shield.analyze`, `shield.analyze_batch`, `shield.redact`, `shield.unredact`, `shield.profiles` — that any MCP client (Claude Desktop, Goose, Cursor) can call. The redact / unredact pair lets you mask identifying entities before sending text to a cloud LLM and restore them on the response, byte-for-byte. Layer 3 calls localhost Ollama only — never an external endpoint.

profiles.title: Active profiles
profiles.description: Comma-separated list of Shield profile ids to load. Available: shield-legal, shield-therapy, shield-therapy-pro, shield-finance.

tools.name: shield.analyze
tools.description: Classify text for regulatory sensitivity. Returns score (0-100), level (NONE..CRITICAL), routing suggestion (LOCAL_ONLY / REDACT_CLOUD / CLOUD_OK), detected entities (shape-only by default), and pipeline diagnostics.

tools.name: shield.analyze_batch
tools.description: Analyze multiple texts in parallel. Per-item failures are captured (one bad input doesn't sink the rest of the batch).

tools.name: shield.redact
tools.description: Substitute identifying entities with deterministic tokens. Use before sending text to a cloud LLM. Numbers, percentages, and ratios survive; names, firms, emails, and IDs get masked.

tools.name: shield.unredact
tools.description: Restore tokens in text to their original values using the mapping returned by shield.redact. Round-trip is byte-for-byte.

tools.name: shield.profiles
tools.description: List available shield profiles loaded on this server. Use to discover what categories each profile covers.
